---
title: "Relational Databases and SQL Systems Review"
subtitle: "Relations, Keys, Constraints, Normalization, Transactions, Indexes, Query Workloads, and Governance"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Relational design objective

Define the domain relations, table grains, keys, constraints, dependencies, transaction boundaries, indexes, and access controls.

## Reproducible example

```{sql}
SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name;
```

## Governance review

Check primary keys, foreign keys, nullability, uniqueness, check constraints, normalization risks, query workload fit, transaction evidence, and access controls.

## Appendix

Attach schema inventory, constraints, workloads, indexes, normalization checks, transaction logs, access controls, and integrity incidents.
